prompt: The dispatcher is requeuing jobs the moment they're handed out. Pattern from the logs: job A's deadline lapses while the loop is busy (nobody polled the watchdog yet), job A completes anyway and gets cancelled, job B is armed — and the very next Expired() poll claims job B timed out, zero ticks after arming, with job B's label on the report. Same thing when a lapsed job is simply replaced by the next dispatch without a cancel in between. Deadlines that are armed and genuinely lapse work fine, cancels keep cancelled jobs quiet, and each expiry only reports once — it's specifically the arm-right-after-a-lapsed-deadline case that fires instantly. The watchdog drives everything through the injected Timer abstraction, and our fake in the tests mirrors time.Timer's documented channel semantics exactly, so the bug reproduces deterministically. Fix deadline.go so a re-armed watchdog only ever reports the deadline of the job it is currently guarding — exactly one notification per arm. deadline_test.go is protected. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. Repro (full suite) fails the protected assertion in TestRearmAfterLapsedCancelDoesNotReplay: immediately after re-arming for a fresh job the watchdog reports "job-new" as expired because the previous job's undrained tick is still queued (Stop returned false and nothing drained the channel). TestRearmOverAnUnpolledLapsedJob fails the same way without an intervening Cancel.
